CREATE TABLE employees (EMPLOYEE_ID integer PRIMARY KEY, NAME varchar, START_DATE date);
INSERT INTO employees (EMPLOYEE_ID, NAME, START_DATE) VALUES (1, 'John Doe', '2023-01-01');
INSERT INTO employees (EMPLOYEE_ID, NAME, START_DATE) VALUES (2, 'Jane Smith', NULL);

CREATE FUNCTION job_history(IN EMPLOYEE_ID integer, IN START_DATE date) RETURNS varchar { RETURN sprintf_or_null('http://demo.openlinksw.com:8890/hr/job_history#%d_%s', EMPLOYEE_ID, CAST(START_DATE AS varchar)); };
SELECT job_history(1, '2023-01-01') AS history FROM employees;
SELECT job_history(2, NULL) AS history FROM employees;
SELECT job_history(e.EMPLOYEE_ID, e.START_DATE) AS history FROM employees e WHERE e.NAME LIKE 'Jane%';
CREATE TABLE job_history_table (ID integer PRIMARY KEY, EMPLOYMENT_HISTORY varchar);
INSERT INTO job_history_table (ID, EMPLOYMENT_HISTORY) VALUES (1, job_history(1, '2023-01-01'));
INSERT INTO job_history_table (ID, EMPLOYMENT_HISTORY) VALUES (2, job_history(2, NULL));
SELECT j.ID, j.EMPLOYMENT_HISTORY FROM job_history_table j WHERE j.EMPLOYMENT_HISTORY IS NOT NULL;
SELECT job_history(e.EMPLOYEE_ID, e.START_DATE), COUNT(*) FROM employees e GROUP BY e.EMPLOYEE_ID HAVING COUNT(e.EMPLOYEE_ID) > 0;
